data.title: Meta Platforms Inc. cuts jobs, leading shares to rise; CEO Zuckerberg's wealth also increases. Stock closed at $661.53, opened at $664.50, hit high of $676.10, closed at $673.07, up 1.7%. Layoffs possible in January with 30% budget cuts for metaverse unit, which lost over $70 billion since 2021.
data.description: On Thursday, Meta Platforms Inc's (NASDAQ: META) shares rose following an announcement of job cuts within the company. The surge in stock prices saw Meta CEO and co-founder Mark Zuckerberg's wealth increase. The stock opened higher on Thursday at $664.50, moving from a previous close of $661.53 to reach an intraday high of $676.10, ultimately closing at $673.07, marking a 1.7% increase. The positive trend continued into Friday, possibly influenced by the news. Reports suggest that Meta may initiate layoffs as early as January, with a projected 30% reduction in the budget for the metaverse group. Notably, the metaverse unit has incurred losses exceeding $70 billion since 2021. This shift in focus signifies a significant transformation for the company.

data.title: Corporate pension funding levels increased in November for 8th month: PFI funded ratio rose to 107.1%, discount rates up to 5.34%.
data.description: In Seattle, Milliman, Inc., a leading global consulting and actuarial firm, unveiled its latest update on the Milliman 100 Pension Funding Index (PFI). This index delves into the financial status of the top 100 U.S. corporate pension plans. In November, the PFI's funded ratio saw a slight increase from 107.0% in October to 107.1% by November 30. The uptick was influenced by a 1-basis-point climb in discount rates, rising to 5.34% in November. This adjustment led to a $3 billion reduction in plan liabilities, counterbalancing market returns of only 0.44%.

data.title: Bitcoin traders remain cautious as risk-off signals trigger despite optimism about a sub-$100K BTC being a bargain. Is $100K feasible by 2025’s end?

data.title: Law firm Faruqi & Faruqi, LLP investigates Bitdeer Technologies for investors suffering losses between June 6, 2024 and November 10, 2025. Contact Partner James Wilson for details.
data.description: Securities Litigation Partner James (Josh) Wilson from Faruqi & Faruqi, LLP is urging investors who have incurred losses in Bitdeer to reach out to him directly to explore potential avenues. If you bought or obtained securities in Bitdeer during the period from June 6, 2024, to November 10, 2025, and wish to discuss your options, please contact Mr. Wilson.
